task_completion.description: Verify that a private static final Logger named LOGGER is initialized in the Java classes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the open Java files in the Eclipse editor. You should see 'import java.util.logging.Logger;' and a class-level declaration like 'private static final Logger LOGGER = Logger.getLogger(ClassName.class.getName());'.

task_completion.description: Verify that System.out.println calls have been replaced with LOGGER.info.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Eclipse editor or search results. Standard output prints should be replaced with 'LOGGER.info(...);'.

task_completion.description: Verify that System.err.println calls have been replaced with LOGGER.severe.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Eclipse editor or search results. Error prints should be replaced with 'LOGGER.severe(...);'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the final System.out.println in Main.java is preserved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at Main.java in the editor. Near the end of the file, there should still be a 'System.out.println' call printing the pipeline result summary (e.g., PIPELINE_RESULT=SUCCESS), not a LOGGER call.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project compiles and runs successfully after the migration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Eclipse Console view or a terminal window showing the execution output of the DataPipeline project. The output should display standard java.util.logging timestamps and log levels (INFO, SEVERE) without compilation errors.

task_completion.description: Verify that migration_report.txt was created and lists the number of modified files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'migration_report.txt' open in an editor or its contents displayed in a terminal (e.g., via 'cat'). It should contain the number '10' indicating the number of files modified.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Eclipse IDE to perform or verify the migration.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE should be visible in the screenshots with the DataPipeline project open in the Package Explorer, and Java files open in the editor.

integrity.description: Verify the execution output genuinely reflects the java.util.logging format.
integrity.visual_evidence: The console output should show standard Java logging formats (e.g., 'Jan 01, 2024 12:00:00 PM com.datapipeline... INFO:') rather than just plain text, proving the logger is actually running.

integrity.description: Verify the files were actually modified and not just a report generated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse editor tabs should show an asterisk (*) indicating unsaved changes during the process, or the search view should show the actual replaced lines in the filesystem.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/DataPipeline. There are exactly 10 Java files that need to be modified. After a correct migration, there should be approximately 34 Logger calls (30 LOGGER.info and 4 LOGGER.severe) across the project. The final System.out.println in Main.java must be preserved. The migration report at /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/DataPipeline/migration_report.txt should state that 10 files were modified.
